message.content:
Several brands offer reliable, well‑rounded tool ranges that cover home repair, DIY projects, and gardening. The best choice can depend on your budget, how often you’ll use the tools, and whether you prefer corded, battery‑powered, or manual tools. Here are some strong options:

• DeWalt – Durable, pro‑grade power tools with an extensive lineup of cordless (20V/60V) tools and some outdoor/garden tools. Good for serious DIYers.  
• Makita – High‑quality tools with a very wide selection, including power tools, outdoor power equipment, and garden tools. Known for reliability.  
• Ryobi – Excellent balance of price and performance. Their 18V ONE+ system covers hundreds of tools for home repair, DIY, and gardening. Great for homeowners.  
• Bosch – Strong in power tools and DIY equipment, plus a solid range of gardening tools (more prominent in Europe). Reliable and user‑friendly.  
• Black+Decker – Affordable, beginner‑friendly tools for casual home use and light gardening. Good for basic DIY.  
• Milwaukee – Premium professional‑grade tools with outdoor equipment as well. High quality but higher cost.  
• Husqvarna – If your focus includes heavier gardening or yard maintenance (mowers, trimmers, chainsaws), this brand is excellent.

If you want the best all‑around value for a single ecosystem, Ryobi is often the top choice for homeowners because one battery platform powers almost everything. If you prefer higher professional quality, Makita and DeWalt are the usual go‑tos.

If you tell me your budget or the type of projects you plan to do, I can recommend more specifically.
